title: Extraction and purification of viruses from stream biofilms
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bimjd","text":"This series of sequential protocols can be used to extract and purify viruses from stream biofilm samples for metagenomic analysis. We compared each critical step of the protocol to alternatives and evaluated their performance using epifluorescence counting of viral-like particles and DNA yields. The pipeline consists of concentration of sample using tangential flow filtration, extraction using tetrasodium pyrophosphate and sonication and purfication using DNase I treatment, filtration, and ultracentrifugation in a sucrose density gradient. Where appropriate, we provide links to similar protocols.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Preparations - 200 x TE Buffer: 2M Tris-HCl (pH 8.5) + 0.2M EDTA. - 1 x TE buffer: 10 mM Tris + 1 mM EDTA (pH 8.0). - 10 x DNase buffer: 100 mM Tris (pH 7.5) + 5mM CaCl 2 + 25 mM MgCl 2 .
Centrifuge biofilm sample at 100 x g for 15 minutes at 4°C. 00:15:00
Use TFF to concentrate viruses following instruction posted here: https://www.protocols.io/view/Phage-Tangential-Flow-Filtration-c7yzpv/

Compare to protocol: https://www.protocols.io/view/SYBR-Green-or-Gold-Staining-c7sznd Because of high background noise in biofilm samples, it is important that VLP are counted after purification and DNase I treatment.
Add 0.1 volumes of 200 x TE buffer, 0.01 volumes of 0.5M EDTA (pH 8) and 1 volume of formamide to sample. Mix well and incubate at room temperature for 30 minutes. 00:30:00
DNase I digestion Add 0.1 volumes of DNase buffer 10 x.
Add 5 mM (final concentration) of tetrasodium pyrophosphate to the concentrated biofilm sample.
Sucrose gradient ultracentrifugation Compare to protocol posted here: https://www.protocols.io/view/Purifying-Viruses-Using-Sucrose-Cushion-c3wypd
Recover the supernatant for downstream analyses.
Incubate in dark for 15 minutes. 00:15:00
Sonicate the sample on ice three times for 1 minute with 30 second intervals. Shake samples between sonication intervals.
Centrifuge at 3234 x g for 15 minutes at 4°C. 00:15:00
Filter sequentially the supernatant through 0.8 and 0.45 µm filter, respectively.
Add 2.5 U/µL (final concentration) of DNase I enzyme.
Incubate for 3 hours at 37˚C. 03:00:00
Heat-inactivate DNase I enzyme at 65°C for 15 minutes. 00:15:00
Check for microbial contamination with a standard PCR using universal 16S rRNA gene primers and gel electrophoresis. See for instance: https://www.protocols.io/view/16S-PCR-Universal-c5my45
Pipette 3 mL of 66% sucrose and then 7 mL of 30% sucrose into the clean ultracentrifugation tube. Be careful not to mix the gradient.
Mark layers with a permanent marker to facilitate layer recognition.
Overlay the sample on top of the gradient and fill to the top. Be careful not to cause any mixing of the gradient.
Ultracentrifuge at 106800 x g for 2 hours at 4°C. Use a slow acceleration and no brake for the deceleration. 02:00:00
Using an 18 G needle, pierce carefully the tube between the 66% and 30% sucrose layers and extract approximately 2 mL.
Stain sample with 2 µL 1000 x SYBR Gold and incubate for 30 minutes in the dark at room temperature. 00:30:00
Carefully filter the sample onto 0.02 µm pore size membrane filter (Anodisc).
Mount filter on glass slide using a drop of VectaShield.
Screen the filter under fluorescence microscope using blue light excitation (488 nm) and green emission (512 nm).
Add 2 volumes of 100% EtOH.
Incubate at 4°C for 30 minutes. 00:30:00
Centrifuge at 17000 x g for 20 minutes at 4°C to pellet the DNA. 00:20:00
Resuspend DNA with 567 μL of 1 x TE buffer.
Optional stopping point: store the sample at -20°C for up to one month.
Pre-warm SDS at 37°C and CTAB at 65°C.
Add 30 μL of 10% warm SDS and 3 μL of proteinase K (20 mg/mL).
Mix and incubate for 1 hour at 37°C. 01:00:00
Add 100 μL of 5M NaCl and mix.
Add 80 μL of warm CTAB solution.
Vortex and incubate at 65°C for 10 minutes. 00:10:00
Add equal volume of chloroform.
Vortex and centrifuge at 16000 x g for 10 minutes at room temperature. 00:10:00
Transfer top aqueous layer to new microcentrifuge tube.
Add equal volume of 25:24:1 phenol:chloroform:isoamyl alcohol.
Vortex, and centrifuge at 16000 x g for 10 minutes at room temperature. 00:10:00
Transfer top aqueous layer to new microcentrifuge tube.
Add equal volume of chloroform to supernatant.
Vortex, and centrifuge at 16000 x g for 10 minutes at room temperature. 00:10:00
Transfer top aqueous layer to new microcentrifuge tube.
Add 0.7 volumes of isopropanol to supernatant.
Gently mix and incubate at -20°C overnight to precipitate DNA.
Centrifuge at 16000 x g for 20 minutes at 4°C. 00:20:00
Wash the DNA pellet twice with 70% cold EtOH.
Resuspend DNA with 50 μL of DNase-free H 2 O.
Quantify DNA (e.g. using Qubit according to manufacturer's instructions).
Wash the pellet twice with 70% ice cold EtOH and allow to dry at room temperature.
creator: Meriem Bekliz
